title: The Presentation
module_title: Effective Pentest Findings Presentation to Diverse Audiences
learning_objectives: Create compelling executive presentations focused on business impact
Develop detailed technical presentations for engineering teams
Anticipate and prepare answers to difficult questions
key_topics: Executive presentation design (10 slides, business focus)
scenario: CTO wants presentations: 30 min for executives, 1 hour for engineers. Prepare both and anticipate tough questions.

scenario_context.setting: Post-pentest findings presentation to mixed audiences
scenario_context.challenge: Tailor message to executive and technical audiences
scenario_context.relevance: Presentation skill determines buy-in for remediation

subtasks.title: Executive Presentation
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Create 10-slide presentation: title, overview, key findings (business impact), risk timeline, recommendations, budget/timeline. Heavy visuals, minimal text.
subtasks.evidence_required: Executive slide deck (10 slides, professional design)

subtasks.title: Technical Presentation
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Create 20+ slide technical presentation covering: methodology, findings (technical detail), exploitation demos, remediation steps, timeline.

subtasks.title: Q&A Preparation
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Prepare 5 tough questions per audience (10 total). Write substantive answers demonstrating expertise and anticipating concerns.

subtasks.title: Attack Narrative
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Write 1-page narrative describing the complete attack story from initial access through compromise. Make it compelling and understandable.

subtasks.title: Delivery Practice Notes
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Create detailed delivery notes for both presentations: timing per slide, key points, speaking notes, emphasis, transitions.
subtasks.evidence_required: Delivery notes for executive and technical presentations

reflection_prompt: How would you handle a question you couldn't answer in the presentation? What audience reaction would indicate successful communication?

quiz.question: What should dominate an executive presentation?
quiz.explanation: Executives care about business impact, remediation cost, timeline, and risk reduction. Technical details distract from key messages.

quiz.question: Why is attack narrative storytelling valuable?
options: Makes complex attacks understandable and memorable
quiz.explanation: Narrative storytelling makes technical attacks relatable and memorable, helping audiences understand the severity of findings.

portfolio_artifacts: Q&A document (10 questions with comprehensive answers)
mentor_notes: Evaluate presentation quality and audience-appropriate customization. Executive deck should be visually compelling with minimal text. Technical deck should enable remediation. Q&A should demonstrate expertise. Narrative clarity is important.
